rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM THOMAS VS. KENNETH ROBERSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 22, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANT PATRICIA ORLANDO'S MOTION TO STRIKE. GRANT WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE GRAVAMEN OF THE COMPLAINT IS SLANDER ARISING FROM DEFENDANT'S COMMUNICATIONS WITH HER SON'S PSYCHOLOGIST AND PEDIATRICIAN. THE COMMUNICATIONS WERE MADE DURING EXAMINATIONS PERFORMED FOR THE PURPOSE OF SEEKING ADVICE AND RECOMMENDATIONS FOR USE IN THE FAMILY COURT PROCEEDINGS. ALL OF THE COMMUNICATIONS ARE CONSTITUTIONALLY PROTECTED SPEECH UNDER CCP SECTION 425.16(e)(2). PLAINTIFF FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE A LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING AT TRIAL. DEFENDANT'S STATEMENTS ARE PRIVILEDGED UNDER CC SECTION 47(b)(4), THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE, BECAUSE THEY WERE MADE IN THE INITIATION OR COURSE OF A FAMILY COURT PROCEEDING. =(302/CWW)
